meta.label: Ghanaian
meta.description: Bold, savory Ghanaian cooking anchored by pepper-forward sauces, palm oil depth, aromatic ginger-garlic bases, fermented corn/cassava staples, smoky fish, and comforting soups/stews served with starches.
heritageNotes: Commonly served as soups and stews paired with starches (swallows, rice, kenkey, banku).
Pepper sauces (shito, fresh pepper blends) and smoked fish are frequent flavor anchors.
Fermentation is culturally important in staples like kenkey and banku.
meta.author: Suka Smart Assistant

source.attribution: Curated profile; traditional Ghanaian patterns summarized for SSA planning.
source.author: Suka Smart Assistant
source.notes: Designed as a planner-oriented cuisine profile with pantry tiers, archetypes, and substitution guidance.

overlapsWith.reason: Shared West African pepper bases, palm oil usage, fish/seafood integration, and stew-and-starch serving patterns.
Overlap in pepper-forward stews, seafood usage, and layered aromatics, though seasoning blends differ.

subprofiles.name: Akan (Ashanti/Fante)
subprofiles.signatureNotes: Groundnut soup, palm nut soup, light soup, and fish/seafood stews; often paired with fufu or rice.

subprofiles.name: Ga-Dangme (Greater Accra)
subprofiles.signatureNotes: Kenkey and fish, pepper sauces, and okro stews; strong coastal fish traditions.

subprofiles.name: Ewe (Volta)
subprofiles.signatureNotes: Banku and tilapia, pepper sauces, and greens-forward soups/stews; smoked fish is frequent.

notes: Dish archetype defaults must only use allowed keys from schema (no fatOptions/sweetBalanceOptions/spiceOptions).

plannerDefaults.weeknightBias: One-pot soups/stews and quick pepper sauces; rely on smoked fish and pantry bases for speed.

batchCookingBias.notes: Many Ghanaian soups and sauces freeze well and reheat successfully; batch bases improve weeknight execution.

signatureNotes: Pepper-forward sauces (fresh pepper blends and shito) balanced by aromatics and savory depth from smoked fish or dried seafood.
Palm oil and groundnut-based soups provide richness; ginger-garlic-onion is a common aromatic backbone.
Fermented staples (kenkey, banku) and smoked proteins create a layered, comforting profile.

tier0_minimum.description: Minimum to cook Ghanaian-adjacent meals with common grocery staples and simple substitutions.

tier1_core.description: Core pantry that unlocks the most common Ghanaian soups, stews, and pepper sauces with authentic direction.

tier2_traditional.description: Traditional staples and hard-to-find items that enable more region-specific cooking and deeper authenticity.

tier3_specialty.description: Specialty items for advanced fidelity, ceremonial dishes, and niche regional preparations.

notes: Pepper bases are frequently blended and cooked down to remove rawness.
Soups are built by simmering proteins first, then enriching the broth with tomato/pepper or groundnut/palm nut bases.
Starch pairings are treated as a co-equal component (soup-and-swallow pattern).

dishArchetypes.label: Soup and swallow
dishArchetypes.description: A deep, savory soup (groundnut, palm nut, light soup, or okro) paired with a starch base such as fufu, banku, or kenkey.
commonExamples: Palm nut soup + rice balls/omo tuo

dishArchetypes.label: Stew and rice
dishArchetypes.description: Tomato-pepper stew or jollof-style rice served with protein and optional fried plantains.

dishArchetypes.label: Grill and pepper sauce
dishArchetypes.description: Charred/grilled protein (often fish) served with fresh pepper sauce and a starch (banku/kenkey/rice).

dishArchetypes.label: Beans and plantain
dishArchetypes.description: Beans/legumes paired with plantains and spicy condiments; hearty, budget-friendly, and meal-prep friendly.

dishArchetypes.label: Breakfast porridge
dishArchetypes.description: Warm porridges and drinks (e.g., koko) often paired with bread or small bites.

avoid_or_limit.label: Heavy cream
avoid_or_limit.reason: Not typical in Ghanaian savory profiles; can mute pepper-and-smoke character. If needed, use small amounts of coconut milk in specific dishes.

avoid_or_limit.label: Excess soy sauce
avoid_or_limit.reason: Not a core seasoning driver; bouillon/dried seafood and long simmering provide the expected umami.

proteinSubstitutions.notes: Goat is classic for many soups; beef/lamb give similar depth; chicken shortens cook time.
If smoked fish is unavailable, use firm fresh fish and reinforce umami with dried shrimp or a small amount of bouillon.

sideSubstitutions.notes: Kenkey/banku are fermented; if unavailable, serve soup/stew with rice, boiled yam, or a simple cornmeal starch.

thickenerSubstitutions.notes: Palm nut concentrate is distinct; when missing, shift to palm-oil pepper stew or groundnut soup patterns rather than forcing an imitation.
Natural peanut butter works well; avoid sweetened peanut butter.

notes: Ghanaian cooking tolerates practical substitutions as long as the aromatic base (onion/ginger/garlic), pepper heat, and umami (smoked fish/dried seafood) remain present.

mealTemplates.label: Groundnut soup + fufu

components.notes: Groundnut soup often starts with a tomato/pepper base then enriches with groundnut paste.
Goat or beef for depth; chicken for faster weeknight version.
Fufu is often a cassava/plantain blend; yam-based swallow also pairs well.
Serve pepper sauce on the side to let diners control heat.

prepNotes: Batch cook groundnut base and freeze in 2-cup portions.
Cook protein first to build stock; add base after meat is tender.

mealTemplates.label: Grilled fish + banku/kenkey + pepper

components.notes: Tilapia is classic; any firm fish works.
Banku/kenkey if available; rice is the common fallback.
Fresh pepper sauce with onions and citrus is the hallmark pairing.

prepNotes: Blend pepper sauce and store 3–4 days refrigerated.
Marinate fish with aromatics; grill hot for charred edges.

mealTemplates.label: Red-red (beans stew) + fried plantain

components.notes: Use cooked beans; stew builds flavor with palm oil and pepper base.
Ripe plantains are key for sweet balance.

prepNotes: Batch cook beans; refrigerate or freeze in meal portions.
Fry plantains fresh for best texture; air-fry is an acceptable shortcut.

safetyNotes.label: Smoked fish and dried seafood bones
notes: Rinse smoked fish and dried seafood; remove visible bones before adding to soups/stews.
When serving, warn diners if small bones may remain.

safetyNotes.label: Hot pepper handling
notes: Use gloves when handling scotch bonnet/habanero; avoid touching eyes/face.
For milder heat, add pepper whole and remove after simmering.

safetyNotes.label: Palm oil storage
notes: Store palm oil sealed away from heat and light to reduce rancidity.
If palm oil smells bitter or stale, discard.

availabilityNotes.note: Palm nut concentrate may be hard to find; favor palm-oil tomato stews and groundnut soups when unavailable.
Kontomire (cocoyam leaves) can be substituted with spinach or a spinach/collards mix.
Kenkey/banku require fermented dough; cornmeal starch or rice are common fallbacks outside West Africa.

hallmarkProcesses.name: Pepper base reduction
hallmarkProcesses.notes: Reducing blended pepper/tomato removes rawness and concentrates sweetness before adding proteins or thickeners.

hallmarkProcesses.name: Soup build with protein stock
hallmarkProcesses.notes: Many Ghanaian soups begin with simmered protein to build a flavorful stock before enrichment.

notes: A blender is central for pepper bases and soup foundations.
Charcoal grilling creates hallmark smoky flavor for fish and meats.
Pressure cooker speeds up goat/beef for soups.
Mortar and pestle is useful for traditional pepper and spice pastes.

dietCompatibility.gluten_free: Many core dishes are gluten-free when paired with GF starches; confirm processed bouillon and blends.
dietCompatibility.dairy_free: Most savory dishes are naturally dairy-free.
dietCompatibility.vegetarian: Possible with beans/groundnut soups and vegetable stews; maintain umami with mushrooms/fermented seasonings.

core.name: Groundnut Soup
core.notes: Peanut-forward soup often built on a pepper-tomato base with goat, chicken, or beef.

core.name: Light Soup
core.notes: Peppery, aromatic soup often served with fufu.

core.name: Palm Nut Soup
core.notes: Rich palm fruit soup; often paired with rice balls (omo tuo) or fufu.

core.name: Red-Red (Beans Stew)
core.notes: Beans stew commonly served with fried ripe plantains.

core.name: Waakye
core.notes: Rice and beans meal served with shito and assorted sides.

core.name: Grilled Tilapia with Banku/Kenkey and Pepper
core.notes: Charred fish with fermented starch and fresh pepper sauce.

dessertsAndDrinks.name: Hausa Koko (Spiced Millet Porridge)
dessertsAndDrinks.notes: Warm breakfast porridge often flavored with ginger and spices.

typicalFlavorPairings.note: Groundnut soup pairs best with fufu/swallow textures that carry the rich broth.
Fresh pepper sauce, onions, and citrus brighten grilled fish and fermented starches.
Sweet fried plantains balance the savory beans; shito adds smoky heat.

antiPairings.note: Creamy dairy-forward sauces generally clash with Ghanaian pepper-smoke profiles; prefer coconut-based richness only where culturally appropriate.

ingredientRules.notes: Use unsweetened natural peanut butter as the closest widely-available substitute.
Spinach is an accessible substitute; mix with collards for a more robust greens profile.
Shift toward palm-oil tomato stew or groundnut soup styles rather than attempting an exact palm nut soup replication.

methodRules.notes: Use broiler for char; finish with a hot pan sear if needed.

batchFriendly.name: Shito (black pepper sauce) batch
batchFriendly.notes: Make a large batch; store refrigerated in small jars or freeze in portions.

batchFriendly.name: Groundnut soup base
batchFriendly.notes: Freeze in 2-cup portions; reheat and add protein/stock as needed.

batchFriendly.name: Light soup pepper base
batchFriendly.notes: Cook down blended pepper base; freeze for quick weeknight soups.

weeknightShortcuts: Keep blended pepper base and shito ready to cut cook time in half.
Use smoked fish and dried shrimp to add depth quickly without long reductions.
Pressure cook goat/beef, then finish soups with pepper base and aromatics on the stove.

notes: Prefer SSA default plate templates that support stew-and-starch and soup-and-swallow patterns.
